title: thymus serpyllum proles chamaedrys
intro: Thymus serpyllum proles chamaedrys, commonly known as wild thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, a low-growing, flowering herb native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Thymus serpyllum proles chamaedrys include its antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it effective against respiratory and gastrointestinal infections. The health benefits of wild thyme include its ability to aid in digestion, alleviate coughs and colds, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Thymus serpyllum proles chamaedrys include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
